title: Strength – Any Diet
plateMethod: Eat enough to fuel training and recovery: protein at each meal (meat, fish, eggs, dairy, or plant-based alternatives), complex carbs around training (rice, oats, potatoes), and healthy fats (nuts, olive oil, avocado). Don’t undereat; moderate surplus supports strength gains.
hydration: Aim for 2.5–3 L of water per day. Drink before, during, and after training. Electrolytes from food (e.g. bananas, salted meals) help if you sweat a lot.
sampleMeals: Breakfast: Eggs with toast and fruit; or oatmeal with milk, nuts, and banana.
Lunch: Lean protein (chicken, fish, tofu, legumes) with rice or potato and vegetables.
Dinner: Similar to lunch—protein, starch, and vegetables. Option for red meat 1–2x per week for iron.
Pre/workout: Carbs + light protein 1–2 hours before (e.g. banana and yogurt).
Post-workout: Protein and carbs within 1–2 hours (e.g. chicken and rice, or a balanced meal).
grocerySuggestions: Eggs, chicken, fish, lean beef (if desired)
Legumes, tofu, Greek yogurt for plant-based
disclaimer: General guidance only; consult a healthcare or nutrition professional for personalized advice, especially if you have medical conditions or dietary restrictions.
